results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE PURPOSE OF THIS PROJECT IS TO IMPROVE THE USE OF LOW-COST SENSORS AND AMBIENT AIR MONITORING TO BETTER QUANTIFY SPATIOTEMPORAL VARIATIONS OF LANDFILL METHANE EMISSIONS.ACTIVITIES:THE SPECIFIC OBJECTIVES ARE: 1) DEVELOP LOW-COST AIR QUALITY SENSORS TO CAPTURE METHANE VARIATION SPATIALLY AND TEMPORALLY; 2) INTEGRATE SENSOR DATA FROM FIXED LOCATIONS, MOBILE SAMPLING, AND DRONE SURVEYS TO MAP POLLUTION DISTRIBUTION AND ESTIMATE EMISSION FLUXES; AND 3) CORRELATE THE SPATIOTEMPORALLY RESOLVED METHANE EMISSION DATA WITH MULTIPLE ENVIRONMENTAL FACTORS FOR EVALUATING METHANE REDUCTION TECHNIQUES.SUBRECIPIENT:NO SUBAWARDS ARE INCLUDED IN THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT.OUTCOMES:DELIVERABLES INCLUDE ANNUAL AND FINAL RESEARCH REPORTS. THE ANTICIPATED OUTPUTS INCLUDE INTEGRATING SAMPLING TECHNOLOGIES FOR METHANE EMISSION QUANTIFICATION. THE DATASETS WILL INFORM LANDFILL OPERATIONS, METHANE EMISSION MODELS AND METHANE REDUCTION TECHNIQUES. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES OF THIS PROJECT INCLUDE COMMUNITIES AFFECTED BY AIR POLLUTANTS EMITTED BY THE LANDFILL STUDY SITES IN IN MIAMI, FLORIDA AS WELL THE GENERAL PUBLIC, WHO ARE AFFECTED BY METHANE'S CONTRIBUTION TO CLIMATE CHANGE.
